summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds the Netmaker server from source and demonstrates that an unauthenticated attacker can create a superadmin account on a fresh deployment, then use that account to access protected admin-only endpoints.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated POST to /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin should require authentication but instead creates a superadmin account, granting full system control to the attacker.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction pipeline built the Netmaker server, confirmed no superadmin existed via the unauthenticated hasSuperAdmin endpoint, created a superadmin account without any authentication, authenticated with the new credentials, and accessed admin-only endpoints successfully.
verification_method: The exploit script first checks GET /api/users/adm/hassuperadmin to confirm the system is in its initial state. It then creates a superadmin via POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin without any authorization header. It authenticates with the new credentials and calls GET /api/users (admin-only) to verify full superadmin access. Success is confirmed when the admin-only endpoint returns user data.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /api/users/adm/hassuperadmin returned false without authentication, confirming fresh deployment state.
POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin returned 200 with the new admin user without any authorization header.
POST /api/users/adm/authenticate with the new credentials returned a valid JWT.
GET /api/users (admin-only) returned the user list, confirming full superadmin access.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
